senses.gloss: to be or become short, to shorten
to fall short of, to miss
to unable, to be incapable of
to desist, to refrain, to abstain
to shorten, to curtail, to pare
to detain, to lock up, to pen up, to put in jail
to hold back, to keep or restrict (within certain limits), to restrain
to confine to, to limit to, to restrict to
to shorten; to abbreviate; to clip (hair)
to fall short, to shirk one's responsibilities, to not pull one's weight
to lag behind, to be slothful
large stone building from which power is projected, castle, palace
to wash, to scour, to full, to walk
to wash, to scour, to full, to walk

etymology.text:
From Aramaic קַצְרָא (qaṣrā), from Latin castrum. Cognate to Classical Syriac ܩܰܨܪܳܐ (qaṣrā), Hebrew קְצָרָה (qəṣārâ), Byzantine Greek κάστρον (kástron).

Back-formed from قَصَّار (qaṣṣār, “walker, fuller”).
